pattern: A sliding window whose cost mixes a max charge with a length-scaled running-cost sum stays affordable by shrinking from the left, with a monotonic deque tracking the window maximum in amortized linear time.
hints: The cost of a consecutive block uses the largest charge time in it plus the block length times the sum of running costs.
Grow a window to the right; when its cost exceeds the budget, drop elements from the left until it fits again, and record the best width seen.
Maintain the window's running-cost sum directly and its maximum charge time with a decreasing deque so each window cost is O(1) amortized.

edge_inputs.description: first three robots fit the budget
high charge in the middle limits the window
length scaling makes a long window too costly

stress.description: random charges and running costs with a mid-range budget
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    charge = [rng.randrange(1, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    running = [rng.randrange(1, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    budget = rng.randrange(1, 5000000)
    return [charge, running, budget]
stress.note: varied budget exercises growing and shrinking windows
